rationale_for_baseline_tier: A high-energy motor vehicle collision with an unconscious, entrapped, exsanguinating driver in Class IV haemorrhagic shock with airway compromise is an immediate threat to life. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) — upgraded to a Priority 1 aeromedical / intensive-care paramedic response given entrapment, the severity, and the rural location — is the only appropriate tier. Every minute of delay increases mortality from airway obstruction and exsanguination.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening or absent breathing / gurgling / cannot maintain an open airway
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — while 000 is on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: Airway obstruction in an obtunded trauma patient is minutes-to-death; open the airway with a gentle jaw thrust maintaining spinal alignment and keep 000 on the line for guidance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his breathing gets worse or noisy, gently lift his jaw forward without tilting his head back to open the airway, keep his neck still, and tell the 000 operator straight away — they will talk you through it.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Ongoing heavy bleeding / increasing pallor / deterioration from shock
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately and continuously until paramedics arrive
escalation_edges.rationale: Uncontrolled external and internal haemorrhage drives the shock; firm sustained direct pressure on the external wound is the only bystander lever while blood products and surgery are mobilised.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Press hard on the head wound with a cloth and do not stop. If it soaks through, put another cloth on top and keep pressing. Keep him warm. Tell 000 he is very pale and bleeding heavily.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of pulse / not breathing at all (traumatic cardiac arrest)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — begin CPR after rapid extrication to the ground
escalation_edges.rationale: If the patient arrests, chest compressions require him flat on a firm surface; a rapid takedown to the ground and dispatcher-guided CPR is the correct action, though survival from traumatic arrest is poor.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he stops breathing or you can't feel a pulse, tell 000 immediately. With help, carefully move him to the ground and start pushing hard and fast in the centre of his chest — the operator will count you through it.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Rural/remote location — standard road ambulance response times are long
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Escalate explicitly to an aeromedical / HEMS retrieval and intensive-care paramedic response, and request blood products on scene; prolonged pre-hospital time makes bystander haemorrhage control and airway protection even more critical.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Care depends entirely on a distressed, untrained lay bystander
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give one clear instruction at a time, keep the bystander on the line, and prioritise the two highest-yield lay actions — direct pressure and keeping the airway/patient still — over anything technical.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 right now and ask for an ambulance — tell them it is a high-speed car crash into a tree, the driver is trapped, unconscious and bleeding heavily, so they send the fastest possible response including a rescue helicopter. Press hard on the head wound and don't stop. Do not move him unless there's fire or he stops breathing. Keep him warm, stay clear of the leaking fluid, and clear the driveway for the trucks.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately and stay on the line
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The receiving trauma centre will generate the definitive record; no GP documentation step applies at the scene.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Anything below an immediate 000 emergency ambulance response for an unconscious, exsanguinating, entrapped major-trauma patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Death from airway obstruction or haemorrhagic shock before definitive care is reached.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Directing to ED by self/other transport rather than an emergency ambulance — an entrapped, unstable patient cannot be self-transported and needs pre-hospital resuscitation and extrication.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Exact vital signs and internal injuries cannot be known remotely, and the cause of the crash (mechanical vs a preceding medical event) is unknown.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the visible life threats and escalate maximally — uncertainty about details never lowers the tier when the mechanism and appearance already signal major trauma.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to gather more information, or down-triaging because a diagnosis is not certain.
